questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Figure shows an automobile circuit. How much power (in watt) is dissipated by the automobile circuit when switches $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{C}$ and D are all closed.
questions.solution:
All the elements of circuit are in parallel arrangement
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{1}{R_{e q}}=\frac{1}{40}+\frac{1}{40}+\frac{1}{40}+\frac{1}{40}+\frac{1}{20}+\frac{1}{20}=\frac{4}{40}+\frac{2}{20} ; \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{eq}}=5 \Omega ; \text { Power }=\mathrm{V}^2 / \mathrm{R}=5 \mathrm{~W} \\
& \mathrm{~m}=\frac{3360}{4200}=0.8 \mathrm{~kg} \\
& \text { vol. }=\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{p}}=\frac{0.8}{1000} \mathrm{~m}^3=0.8 l
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the circuit shown in the Figure, cell is ideal and $R_2=100 \Omega$. A voltmeter of internal resistance $200 \Omega$ reads $V_{12}=4 \mathrm{~V}$ and $\mathrm{V}_{23}=6 \mathrm{~V}$ between the pair of points $1-2$ and $2-3$ respectively. What will be the reading of the voltmeter between the points 1-3.(mark the anser in $V$)
questions.solution:
Let emf of the cell be $E$.
Current through the voltmeter (when connected between 1-2) is $\frac{V_{12}}{R_V}=\frac{\mathbf{4}}{\mathbf{2 0 0}}=\frac{\mathbf{1}}{\mathbf{5 0}} \mathrm{A}$

Current through $R_1=\frac{4}{R_1}$
$\therefore$ Current through $R_2=\frac{4}{R_1}+\frac{1}{50}$
$\therefore$ Potential difference across
$R_2=\left(\frac{4}{R_1}+\frac{1}{50}\right) \times 100=\frac{400}{R_1}+2$
$\therefore E=4+\left(\frac{400}{R_1}+2\right)=6+\frac{400}{R_1}$

When the voltmeter is connected between 2-3
Current through voltmeter $=\frac{6}{200} A$
Current through $R_{\mathbf{2}}=\frac{\mathbf{6}}{\mathbf{1 0 0}} \mathrm{A}$
$\therefore$ Current through
$\begin{aligned}
& R_1=\frac{6}{200}+\frac{6}{100}=\frac{18}{200}=\frac{9}{100} A \\
\therefore & E=\frac{9 R_1}{100}+6
\end{aligned}$
From (1) and (2)
$\frac{9 R_1}{100}+6=6+\frac{400}{R_1}$
$\Rightarrow R_1=\sqrt{\frac{40000}{9}}=\frac{200}{3} \Omega$
Put this in (2)
$E=\frac{\mathbf{9}}{\mathbf{1 0 0}} \times \frac{\mathbf{2 0 0}}{\mathbf{3}}+\mathbf{6}=\mathbf{1 2} \mathrm{V}$
When connected across $1-3$, the voltmeter will $\operatorname{read} E=12 \mathrm{~V}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The circuit diagram given in the figure shows the experimental setup for the measurement of unknown resistance by using a meter bridge. The wire connected between the points P \& $\mathrm{Q}(\mathrm{PQ}=100 \mathrm{~cm})$ has uniform cross-sectional area and its resistivity is directly proportional to the distance from point $P$. Null point is obtained with the jockey $J$ with $R_1$ and $R_2$ in the given position. On interchanging the positions $R_1$ and $R_2$ in the gaps the jockey has to be displaced through a distance $\Delta$ from the previous position along the wire to establish the null point. If the ratio of $R_1 / R_2=3$, find the value of $\Delta$ (in cm$)$. Ignore any end corrections. $[$ Take $\sqrt{3}=1.7]$
questions.solution:
From the given circuit diagram $\frac{R_1}{R_2}=\frac{x^2}{(100)^2-x^2} \Rightarrow x=85$
Also $\frac{R_2}{R_1}=\frac{y^2}{(100)^2-y^2} \Rightarrow y=50$
$\Delta=(85-50) \mathrm{cm}=35 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the circuit shown in figure $A B$ is a uniform wire of length $L=5 \mathrm{~m}$. It has a resistance of $2 \Omega / \mathrm{m}$. When $A C=2.0 \mathrm{~m}$, it was found that the galvanometer shows zero reading when switch $s$ is placed in either of the two positions 1 or 2. Find the emf $\mathrm{E}_1$. (mark the answer in $V$)
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& V_{A C}=E_{\mathbf{3}}=\mathbf{3 0} \mathrm{V} \\
& V_{C B}=E_{\mathbf{2}} \\
& \therefore \frac{E_{\mathbf{2}}}{\mathbf{3 0}}=\frac{V_{C B}}{V_{A C}} \\
& \frac{E_{\mathbf{2}}}{\mathbf{3 0}}=\frac{\mathbf{3}}{\mathbf{2}} \quad \quad\left[\because \frac{V_{C B}}{V_{A C}}=\frac{I_{C B}}{I_{A C}}=\frac{\mathbf{3}}{\mathbf{2}}\right] \\
& \therefore E_{\mathbf{2}}=\mathbf{4 5} \mathrm{V} \\
& \quad V_{A B}=\mathbf{3 0}+\mathbf{4 5}=\mathbf{7 5} \mathrm{V} \\
& \quad R_{A B}=\mathbf{2} \times \mathbf{5}=\mathbf{1 0} \Omega
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Current through $A B$ is $\frac{\mathbf{7 5}}{\mathbf{1 0}}=\mathbf{7 . 5} \mathrm{A}$
$\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad & E_1=\mathbf{7 . 5} \times\left(R_{A B}+R\right) \\
& =\mathbf{1 5 0} \mathbf{V} .
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
